name: PostgreSQL 16
what: Open-source relational database and the canonical SQL backbone for many self-hosted web applications.
solves: System of record for transactional data, identity data, billing metadata, scheduling records, CRM sync state, and analytics-friendly event tables.
users: Engineering teams, product teams, and operators who need ACID semantics, mature tooling, and strong ecosystem support.
fit: Excellent fit. It already aligns with Next.js + Drizzle + Auth.js, and many candidate Hylono apps either require PostgreSQL directly or can use it as their preferred production datastore.
runtime: Single binary database server with WAL, background maintenance, replication options, and a large extension ecosystem.
components: Server process, storage volume, WAL archive/backups, optional connection pooler, metrics exporter, and optional replicas.
deploy: Managed service for site data or dedicated self-hosted instances for app groups that need local control, extension freedom, or network isolation.
deps: Fast persistent storage, reliable backups, monitoring, connection pooling for higher concurrency, and disciplined schema migration workflows.
auth: Native database roles, password or certificate auth, TLS, network controls, and application-scoped credentials.
apis: SQL over standard drivers, admin tooling, logical replication, backups/restores, and exporter endpoints for monitoring.
security: Tier-0 data service. Encrypt in transit, restrict network exposure, separate app credentials, test restores, and treat backup handling as a compliance control.
setup_path: Use official PostgreSQL 16 packages or containers; enable TLS, backups, autovacuum visibility, connection pooling, and metrics before broad app consolidation.
env: POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, storage sizing, WAL retention, backup target credentials, and pooler settings.
persistence: Base backups plus WAL archiving; test restore regularly; keep schema migration discipline per app.
operations: Quarterly minor patching, vacuum/bloat review, replication monitoring, slow query visibility, and capacity checks for connection spikes.
failure_points: Connection exhaustion, slow disks, missing WAL retention, poor backup testing, and shared-cluster blast radius from noisy neighbors.
integration: Backbone for the Hylono Next.js app and a strong fit for ZITADEL, Kong, Cal.com, Medusa, Lago, Metabase, Appsmith, and other app databases.
verify: Decide whether Hylono will centralize multiple app schemas on shared PostgreSQL clusters or isolate high-risk apps into separate instances.
risk: Low technical risk but high blast radius. Treat as a core platform dependency and isolate backups, monitoring, and access control accordingly.
gdpr: Will almost certainly hold customer and business data. EU residency, backup geography, encryption, and retention policies matter.
overlap: None. This is a canonical foundational service.
codex_read_first: Read the release notes, current operational runbooks, and the per-app dependency map before consolidating more workloads onto PostgreSQL.
codex_best_path: Keep the public site on Neon/Postgres if that is already stable; add separate PostgreSQL instances or databases for self-hosted apps that do not belong in the main site database.
codex_never_assume: Never assume one shared PostgreSQL cluster is acceptable for every app. Verify extension needs, upgrade windows, backup RPO/RTO, and tenant isolation.

assets.name: Docker official image
assets.notes: Pragmatic container baseline for self-hosting and local validation.

sources.why: Confirms current PostgreSQL 16 point release researched.
Confirms release date and maintenance status.
